title: Wolfenstein: Youngblood

notes: When I hit Play, the game shows running for a few seconds and stops. I have tried forcing native libraries, using steam runtime and beta runtime; tried disabling esync, d3d11, and forcing d3d11, along with all applicable combinations of those flags.  I have tried forcing vulkan(I think) with the +r_renderAPI 1 flag. I've reinstalled proton, steam, and of course the game itself along with the first launch dependencies. I have also tried switching the native manjaro video drivers to the nvidia-430.36 beta drivers,  updating vulkan, and just about everything else I can think of. Most games I own run with steamplay out of the box, but this one refuses to. My friend was very excited to play the multiplayer, but I just can't get the game to launch :(
title: Wolfenstein: Youngblood

notes:

title: Wolfenstein: Youngblood

notes: Installed latest Vulkan AMD Pro drivers from AUR (https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/vulkan-amdgpu-pro, version 19.30_838629-1) and game works great, no low FPS issues like I (and others here) had before installing latest Vulkan drivers
title: Wolfenstein: Youngblood

notes: Radeon VII user listen!  RADV with LLVM or ACO both have very low frame-rate and are unplayable even on ultra what is far from the highest setting. Install AMDs AMDVLK and set the launch command to "VK_ICD_FILENAMES="/etc/vulkan/icd.d/amd_icd64.json"  %command%" does the trick. The performance is now perfect on the highest possible settings. Note that the Game has an unusual CPU usage with using 1 Core 100%. But this seem to not affect gameplay.
